info.title: Cloudron API
info.description:
# Introduction

Cloudron provides a RESTful API to manage apps, users, groups, domains and other resources.

All of the functionality in the Cloudron Dashboard is available through the API, allowing you to script complex actions.

If you are an app developer, the [Cloudron CLI tool](https://www.npmjs.com/package/cloudron) implements a workflow that allows
you to develop apps on your Cloudron. The CLI tool uses the REST API documented here.

## Requests

The API follows standard HTTP REST API conventions.

|Method|Usage|
|--- |--- |
|GET| Retrieveal operations |
|DELETE| Destroys a resource |
|POST| Create new objects or start a new task. Also used to update parts of a resource |
|PUT| Idempotent operation that replaces a resource |
|PATCH| API does not support PATCH |
|HEAD| Read response headers and get no response |

## HTTP Statuses

The API follows standard HTTP REST API response conventions.

2xx responses indicate success. 4xx responses indicate request errors. 5xx responses indicate server errors.

|Name|Description|
|--- |--- |
|200 | Success. Operation succeeded, response body contains more information |
|204 | Success. Operation succeeded, empty response body |
|401 | Access Denied. Usually caused by missing, invalid or expired token|
|403 | Forbidden. Authenticated but operation not permitted. Usually caused by insufficient role/permissions |
|5xx | Internal Server error |

## Pagination

When listing objects, query parameters `page` and `per_page` can be passed in. Note that these parameters have index
starting from 1.

## API Token

To use the REST API, you must first create an [API token](https://docs.cloudron.io/profile/#api-tokens). This token can be provided
in two ways:
  * Via the request query parameter `?access_token=<token>`
  * Via the Authorization header `Bearer <token>`

API Tokens can be created as `Readonly` or `Read and Write`. With `Readonly` token, only GET operations can be performed.

## cURL Examples

We use `curl` to demonstrate how to use the API. The Cloudron Dashboard Domain and the API Token are variable fields.
You can export them in your shell as follows:

```
export CLOUDRON_DOMAIN=my.domain.com
export CLOUDRON_TOKEN=your_token_here
```

To list the domains using cURL and the `Authorization` header:

```
  curl -H 'ContentType: application/json' -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDRON_TOKEN" https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/domains
```

Alternately, to use the query parameter:

```
  curl -H 'ContentType: application/json' "https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/domains?access_token=$CLOUDRON_TOKEN"
```

# Authorization

<!-- Redoc-Inject: <security-definitions> -->

bearer_auth.description:
To use the Bearer Authentication, you must first create an [API token](https://docs.cloudron.io/profile/#api-tokens).
The API Token can be passed via the Authorization header `Bearer <token>`:
```
  curl -H 'ContentType: application/json' -H 'Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDRON_TOKEN' https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/domains
```

query_auth.name: access_token
query_auth.description:
To use the Query Parameter Authentication, you must first create an [API token](https://docs.cloudron.io/profile/#api-tokens).
The API Token can be passed via the  `access_token` query parameter:
```
  curl -H 'ContentType: application/json' 'https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/domains?access_token=$CLOUDRON_TOKEN'
```

id.description: A unique identifier for the application.

appStoreId.description: The identifier of the application in the app store.

installationState.description: The installation status of the application (e.g., installed, not installed).

error.description: Error message if there is an issue with the application's operation.

runState.description: The current operational state of the application (e.g., running, stopped).

health.description: The health status of the application (e.g., healthy, unhealthy).

taskId.description: The ID of the last task or process run on the application.

accessRestriction.description: Restrictions on access to the application (e.g., admin only).

items.description: Checklist entries for the application.

secondaryDomains.description: Secondary domains associated with the application.

redirectDomains.description: Domains that redirect to the application.

sso.description: Indicates whether the application supports Single Sign-On (SSO).

subdomain.description: The subdomain on which the application runs.

domain.description: The main domain on which the application runs.

fqdn.description: The fully qualified domain name (FQDN) of the application.

certificate.description: The SSL certificate of the application.

manifest.description: Find more about the manifest specification in our knowledgebase.

ports.description: The port bindings of the application.

iconUrl.description: The URL of the application icon.

creationTime.description: The creation date and time of the application.

ts.description: The timestamp of the last update to the application's information.

tags.description: Tags for categorizing and filtering the application.

label.description: A custom label for the application.

enableBackup.description: Indicates whether backups are enabled for the application.

upstreamUri.description: The upstream URI of the application.

accessLevel.description: The access level for the application (e.g., admin, user).

devices.description: Path to devices that the app has access to

status.description: A short identifier corresponding to the HTTP status code returned. For  example, the ID for a response returning a 401 status code would be "Unauthorized".

message.description: A message providing additional information about the error, including  details to help resolve it when possible.

label.description: A custom label for the application.

redirectDomains.description: Domains that redirect to the application.

taskId.description: Use the task id to track the status of the task using the Task API

groups.description: The groupId's of groups that are able to see the external app.

icon.description: This can be an icon, png, jpeg depending on the upstream site. When a custom icon is set, this is the icon which was previously uploaded.
This can be an icon, png, jpeg depending on the upstream site. When a custom icon is set, this is the icon which was previously uploaded.

Footer.description:
The footer is a markdown string. It can be templated with the following variables:
  * `%YEAR%` - the current year
  * `%VERSION%` - current Cloudron version

IpEntries.description: Newline separated list of IP entries. Each entry is an IP address or an IP address range in CIDR notation. Lines starting with `#` are treated as comments.
IpEntries.example:
# This is a IPv4 range
5.75.0.0/16
# This is an IPv6 address
2604:a880:1:4a::2:7000

secret.description: Password for authenticating with the Directory Server

username.description: Username for authenticating with the Docker Registry

email.description: Email for authenticating with the Docker Registry

password.description: Password for authenticating with the Docker Registry

zoneName.description: Specifying the web address for the server.

definitions-provider.description:
The provider for your domain. Available provider:

|Provider |Description |
|--- |--- |
| route53 | AWS Route53 |
| bunny | Bunny |
| cloudflare |Cloudflare |
| digitalocean | DigitalOcean |
| dnsimple | DNSimple |
| gandi | Gandi LiveDNS |
| godaddy |GoDaddy |
| gcdns |Google Cloud DNS |
| hetzner |Hetzner |
| linode |Linode |
| namecom |Name.com |
| namecheap |Namecheap |
| netcup |Netcup |
| ovh | OVH |
| porkbun | Porkbun |
| vultr | Vultr |
| wildcard | Wildcard |
| manual | Manual |
| noop | No-op |

provider.description: The provider of the TLS certificate

wildcard.description: Indicates whether the TLS certificate is a wildcard certificate.

fallbackCertificate.description: Configuration for the fallback TLS certificate.

cert.description: The TLS certificate in PEM format.

key.description: The private key corresponding to the TLS certificate.

config.description: Configuration settings based on the provider.

wellKnown.description:
A `Map` with the following Key and Value.
* The `Key` of the map is the URI scheme as a string
* The `Value` of the map is the content of the URI scheme as a string
Once set, cURL `https://domain/.well-known/$uri-scheme` should return the content.

accessKeyId.description: Access key id for AWS Route53 domain.

secretAccessKey.description: Secret access key for AWS Route53 domain.

tokenType.description: Type of token used for authentication with Cloudflare.

token.description: Token used for authentication with Cloudflare.

email.description: Email associated with the Cloudflare DNS account. Only required when using a "GlobalApiKey" token.

config.description: Specifies config for Digital Ocean DNS.

token.description: Token used for authentication with Digital Ocean, expected to be in ASCII format.
Token used for authentication with deSEC DNS.

accessToken.description: Access token used for authentication with DNSimple DNS.

token.description: Gandi API Key used for authentication with Gandi LiveDNS.

apiKey.description: GoDaddy API Key used for authentication with GoDaddy DNS.

apiSecret.description: Secret key used alongside the API key for authentication with GoDaddy DNS.

jsonFile.description: When Google Cloud DNS is used, a JSON file is expected. The JSON file must include the following properties. Read more about [Google Cloud DNS](https://docs.cloudron.io/domains/#google-cloud-dns).

projectId.description: Project ID for authentication with Google Cloud DNS.

credentials.description: Credentials for authentication with Google Cloud DNS.

token.description: Token used for authentication with Hetzner DNS.
Token used for authentication with Linode DNS.
Token used for authentication with Name.com DNS.
Token used for authentication with Namecheap DNS.

customerNumber.description: Customer ID or account number used for accessing Netcup services.

apiKey.description: API key used for authentication with Netcup DNS.

apiPassword.description: Password used alongside the API key for authentication with Netcup DNS.

endpoint.description: Endpoint URL for OVH DNS API.

appKey.description: Application Key used for authentication with OVH DNS.

appSecret.description: Application Secret used alongside the Application Key for authentication with OVH DNS.

consumerKey.description: Consumer Key generated for accessing OVH services.

apiKey.description: API key used for authentication with Porkbun DNS.

secretApiKey.description: Secret API key used for authentication with Porkbun DNS.

token.description: Token used for authentication with Vultr DNS.

config.description: The config object will be empty when `Wildcard` is used. Read more about Wildcard here: [Wildcard DNS](https://docs.cloudron.io/domains/#wildcard-dns).
The config object will be empty when `Manual` is used. Read more about Manual DNS here: [Manual DNS](https://docs.cloudron.io/domains/#manual-dns).
The config object will be empty when `No-op` is used. Read more about No-op DNS here: [No-op](https://docs.cloudron.io/domains/#no-op-dns).

wellKnown.description:
A `Map` with the following Key and Value.
* The `Key` of the map is the URI scheme as a string
* The `Value` of the map is the content of the URI scheme as a string
Once set, cURL `https://domain/.well-known/$uri-scheme` should return the content.

usernameField.description: LDAP Field to use as the username

filter.description: LDAP Filter to use for filtering users

provider.description: The provider of the email relay service.

banner.description: Specifies the email banner for outgoing emails.

text.description: The text content of the email banner.

html.description: The HTML content of the email banner.

items.description: User mails

ownerId.description: The ID of the owner of the mailbox.

ownerType.description: The type of the owner of the mailbox.

active.description: Indicates whether the mailbox is active.

enablePop3.description: Indicates whether POP3 access is enabled for the mailbox.

storageQuota.description: The storage quota of the mailbox in bytes.

messagesQuota.description: The messages quota of the mailbox.

members.description: An array of user IDs who are members of the mailbox.

items.description: User ID's

membersOnly.description: Indicates whether the mailbox is accessible only to its members.

name.description: The name of the mailbox alias.

domain.description: The domain of the mailbox alias.

items.description: User ID's

messageId.description: ID of the message of the event.

mailbox.description: Mailbox to which the event belongs.

messageId.description: ID of the message of the event.

mailbox.description: Mailbox to which the event belongs.

ip.description: IP address of the remote server.

info.description: Additional information about the remote server.

closed.description: Status indicating whether the connection is closed.

is_private.description: Status indicating whether the connection is private.

is_local.description: Status indicating whether the connection is local.

authUser.description: Authenticated user for the remote server.

Group.description: Groups provide a convenient way to group users. You can assign one or more groups to apps to restrict who can access for an app.

name.description: A label/ display name for a group.

userIds.description: Array of user ids that are part of this group

properties-name.description: A label/ display name for a group.

provider.description:
The provider type determines how IP address is determined for use in IPv4 (A) or IPv6 (AAAA) records Sort order:
  * `noop` - Disable setting DNS record
  * `fixed` - Use static IP for DNS records
  * `interface` - Use the IP address attached to the interface for DNS records
  * `generic` - Automatically detect public IP by calling `cloudron.io`

ip.description: A fixed IPv4 or IPv6 Address

allOf.description: Disables setting of IPv4 or IPv6 DNS records

message.example: To finish ubuntu security updates, a reboot is necessary.

acknowledged.description: Whether notification was acknowledged by user or not

id.description: The unique identifier for the client.

secret.description: The secret key associated with the client.

appId.description: The application ID associated with the client.

loginRedirectUri.description: The URI to redirect to after login.

tokenSignatureAlgorithm.description: The algorithm used to sign tokens.

id.description: The unique identifier for the client.

secret.description: The secret key associated with the client.

appId.description: The application ID associated with the client.

loginRedirectUri.description: The URI to redirect to after login.

tokenSignatureAlgorithm.description: The algorithm used to sign tokens.

fallbackEmail.description: Unique fallback email address. This is the address password reset is sent to. This requires the `password` field to be set to the user's current password.

password.description: When trying to change the `fallbackEmail`, set this field to the user's current password

role.description: User's role determines the resources they have access to

hasBackgroundImage.description: Whether user has a custom dashboard background image

avatarUrl.description:
URL to user's avatar (icon) . This can be:
  * Empty string - if user has no icon set
  * Gravatar URL - external URL if user chose Gravatar
  * Custom Avatar URL - a link to the [custom avatar](#operation/getCustomAvatar) endpoint

uptimeSecs.description: The number of seconds since the system was last restarted.

rebootRequired.description: Indicates whether a system reboot is required.

activationTime.description: The date and time when the product was activated.

model.description: The model name of the CPU.

speed.description: The speed of the CPU in MHz.

type.description: The filesystem type of the device.

mountpoint.description: The mount point of the device.

error.description: If the task errored, this contains the error

active.description: If the task is currently running

pending.description: If the task is scheduled to run

result.description: Result of the task. The fields depends on the type type

ts.description: Time when task was last updated

success.description: If the task succeeded. If it succeeded, the `result` contains the result. Otherwise, `error` contains the error message

name.description: A label/display name for the the token

accessToken.description: The access token that can be used for authentication

identifier.description: Owner of the token. Typically the user id

clientId.description: Client ID for whom the token has been issued

lastUsedAt.description: Time when token was last used

scope.description: Token permissions. This is a map of routes with r (read) or rw (read write) permission

Token-properties-name.description: A label/display name for the the token

scope.description: Token permissions. This is a map of routes with r (read) or rw (read write) permission

version.description: The SemVer Version of the next Cloudron release

sourceTarballSigUrl.description: URL to download release tarball signature. The GPG public key is stored in `src/releases.gpg` of Cloudron git repo.

boxVersionsUrl.description: URL to download release versions file

boxVersionsSigUrl.description: URL to download release versions file signature. The GPG public key is stored in `src/releases.gpg` of Cloudron git repo.

unstable.description: Whether this release is considered stable or not

creationDate.description: Date when this app version was created

publishState.description: Approval status of the app. Usually `approved`.

iconUrl.description: URL to download the app icon for this version

unstable.description: Whether this release is considered stable or not

username.description: Unique username across the instance. The following usernames are reserved: `admin`, `no-reply`, `postmaster`, `mailer-daemon`, `root`, `admins`, `users`.

fallbackEmail.description: Email address for password reset, in case the primary is hosted on the Cloudron itself.

groupIds.description: Array of group ids the user is a member of.

active.description: Indicates if the user is active and can log into apps.

source.description: Indicating if the user is local (empty string) or from an external source like "ldap".

role.description: Role of the user. Can be `admin`, `user`, `usermanager`, `mailmanager` or `owner`.

twoFactorAuthenticationEnabled.description: Indicates if use has enabled two factor authentiation (2FA)

inviteAccepted.description: Indicates if user has finished the invitation to the Cloudron

lockUserProfiles.description: If profile changes by users is disabled

mandatory2FA.description: If two factor authentication for all users is enforced

hostPath.description: Path on host machine where the volume is located.

creationTime.description: Date and time when the volume was created.

mountType.description:
Indicates the method or protocol used to mount a volume, defining how the volume is accessed and interacted with within the system. See more about [mount types](https://docs.cloudron.io/volumes/#mount-type).
  * `cifs`: Network file system protocol commonly used for sharing files and printers in a network.
  * `filesystem`: Refers to the type of volume that is mounted directly from the local filesystem.
  * `ext4`: A filesystem type recognized for its performance, scalability, and support for large file sizes and partitions.
  * `mountpoint`: Represents a location in the filesystem hierarchy where a volume is mounted.
  * `nfs`: Network File System, a distributed file system protocol allowing a user on a client computer to access files over a network as if the files were locally stored.
  * `sshfs`: SSH Filesystem, a network file system protocol built on the Secure Shell protocol.
  * `xfs`: Another type of filesystem known for its scalability and high-performance features.

mountOptions.description: The mountOptions vary based on the chosen mountType. The given example is for the mountType `cifs`

username.description: Username for accessing the volume (if applicable).

password.description: Password for accessing the volume (if applicable).

seal.description: Indicates whether the volume is sealed.

volumeBase.description: Specifies the name and mountType of the volume.

name.description: A valid name for the volume should consist of printable characters and valid filesystem characters. This includes letters, numbers, spaces, and various symbols commonly accepted in filesystem naming conventions.

mountType.description:
Indicates the method or protocol used to mount a volume, defining how the volume is accessed and interacted with within the system.  See more about [mount types](https://docs.cloudron.io/volumes/#mount-type).
  * `cifs`: Network file system protocol commonly used for sharing files and printers in a network.
  * `filesystem`: Refers to the type of volume that is mounted directly from the local filesystem.
  * `ext4`: A filesystem type recognized for its performance, scalability, and support for large file sizes and partitions.
  * `mountpoint`: Represents a location in the filesystem hierarchy where a volume is mounted.
  * `nfs`: Network File System, a distributed file system protocol allowing a user on a client computer to access files over a network as if the files were locally stored.
  * `sshfs`: SSH Filesystem, a network file system protocol built on the Secure Shell protocol.
  * `xfs`: Another type of filesystem known for its scalability and high-performance features.

mountOptions.description: Specifies extra settings for mounting a filesystem. Needed properties vary based on the mountType.

tags.name: Apps
tags.description: Handle your cloudron apps using the apps api.

tags.name: Applinks
tags.description: Applinks are apps that you externally added to your app overview.

tags.name: App Passwords
tags.description: App passwords can be used as a security measure in desktop, email & mobile clients. For example, if you are trying out a new mobile app from an untrusted vendor, you can generate a temporary password that provides access to a specific app. This way your main password does not get compromised (and thus providing access to other apps as well).

tags.name: Backups
tags.description: Handling the backups.

tags.name: Branding
tags.description: The Branding configuration can be used to customize various aspects of the Cloudron like it's name, logo and footer.

tags.name: Cloudron
tags.description: Cloudron Healthcheck route and various global configuration like language & timezone can be found here.

tags.name: Dashboard
tags.description: Configuration of your cloudron server.

tags.name: Docker
tags.description: Docker related configuration.

tags.name: Domains
tags.description: Web addresses for accessing your server and apps. Read more about Cloudron domain settings in our [knowledgebase](https://docs.cloudron.io/domains/).

tags.name: Directory Server
tags.description: Directory Server Configuration

tags.name: Eventlog
tags.description: Cloudron server activity can be monitored using the Eventlog API.

tags.name: External LDAP
tags.description: The External Directory connector allows users from your existing LDAP or active directory to authenticate with Cloudron.

tags.name: Groups
tags.description: Groups provide a convenient way to group users. You can assign one or more groups to apps to restrict who can access for an app.

tags.name: Network
tags.description: Networking and Firewall related configuration.

tags.name: Notifications
tags.description: Cloudron displays notifications in the dashboard for various events like: app down, app out of memory, low disk space, updates available, app update etc. The notifications can be read by clicking on the bell icon in the navigation bar.

tags.name: OIDC
tags.description: OpenID Connect. It allows clients to verify the identity of end-users based on the authentication performed by an authorization server.

tags.name: Profile
tags.description: User's profile information

tags.name: Provision
tags.description: This endpoint allows users to provision and configure domain settings. It validates and applies various configurations such as provider details, domain name, zone information, TLS settings, and optional IPv4 and IPv6 configurations.

tags.name: Reverse Proxy
tags.description: The Reverse Proxy manages the nginx config and TLS certificates.

tags.name: Services
tags.description: Services that are used on your Cloudron instance such as 'mail', 'mysql' and way more.

tags.name: System
tags.description: Server related information.

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: Tasks are asynchronous operations performed in the background. Each task can be monitored separately using the task id.

tags.name: Tokens
tags.description: Tokens are used for API authentication.

tags.name: Updater
tags.description: Updater checks and manages updates of the apps and the platform.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Add, update and remove users from Cloudron User Directory.

tags.name: User Directory

tags.name: Volumes

post.description:
Up to Cloudron v8.1 this route is `POST /apps/install`

Install an app by either providing an app store id or a CloudronManifest. The latter is useful for custom apps.

One of `manifest` or `appStoreId` is required!

appStoreId.description: Cloudron app store ID. Same as the id in the CloudronManifest.json

icon.description: This can be an icon, png, jpeg depending on the upstream site. When a custom icon is set, this is the icon which was previously uploaded.

sso.description: Indicates whether the application supports Single Sign-On (SSO).

enableBackup.description: Indicates whether backups are enabled for the application.

enableAutomaticUpdate.description: Indicates whether automatic updates are enabled.

items.description: Array of userId's that are allowed to access the app.
Array of groupId's that are allowed to access the app.
List of command-line arguments for the debug mode.

readonlyRootfs.description: Indicates if the root filesystem should be mounted as read-only for added security.

overwriteDns.description: Determines whether existing DNS settings should be overwritten during configuration.

skipDnsSetup.description: Skips the DNS setup process if set to true, assuming DNS is already configured.

env.description: Used to set up the app environment. It is an object where the values are strings.

enableMailbox.description: Enable or disable the mailbox feature for the app.

enableTurn.description: Indicates whether TURN (Traversal Using Relays around NAT) is enabled.

upstreamUri.description: The upstream URI of the application.

icon.description: This can be an icon, png, jpeg depending on the upstream site. When a custom icon is set, this is the icon which was previously uploaded.

post.description: 'Owner' role is required to repair app with docker addon.
Check for availabe updates for the app.

updates.description: If the object is empty, there are no available updates.

skipBackup.description: Enable or disable a backup before the app will be updated.

force.description: Enable or disable if the update should be forced.

x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDRON_TOKEN" "https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/apps/$APPID/update" --data '{"appStoreId":"io.n8n.cloudronapp@3.87.0", "skipBackup": true, "force": false}'

backupId.description: ID of the backup used for the restore.

remotePath.description: The path or identifier of the backup used for restoring the app.

backupFormat.description: The format of the backup file. This indicates the structure or encoding used for the backup.

backupConfig.description: Configuration settings for the backup provider used during import.

provider.description: The name of the backup provider (e.g., AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage).

password.description: The password used to access the backup, if it is encrypted.

encryptedFilenames.description: Indicates whether the filenames in the backup are encrypted.

get.description: Get app logs. Use this endpoint to stream logs (`tail -f` style). If you want to download logs, use the [logs](#operation/getLogs) endpoint instead. This endpoint implements [Server Side Events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events). Use the [EventSource](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/EventSource) interface to parse the returned stream.

parameters.name: lines
parameters.description: Last lines displayed.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Defines the format of the logs.

parameters.name: lines
parameters.description: Last lines displayed.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Defines the format of the logs.

schema.description: Each line in the returned logs is of the desired format. When the format is `json`, each line is a [NDJSON](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JSON_streaming#Newline-Delimited_JSON) containing the fields `realtimeTimestamp` and `message`. The `Content-Disposition` header is set to `attachment; filename="box.log"`.

List of email addresses that are blocked (blacklisted). To remove emails from the blacklist, send an empty array.
**Existing emails also have to be inside that array, otherwise blacklist array will be replaced by the the one sent with this call.**

config.description:
The configuration settings for the mail server's spam filter. 
This includes rules and criteria for identifying and blocking spam emails.
The configuration is written in a domain-specific language that utilizes regular expressions
to specify conditions for email addresses that should be blocked or flagged as spam.
It also includes scoring mechanisms to determine the likelihood that an email is spam, 
and descriptions for each rule to explain its purpose and functionality.

Explanation of the example:
| Line                                                                                                             | Explanation                                                                                                                    |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `header TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK To =~ /^(mail@your.domain\\.io\|mail@your.domain\\.org)$/i`                          | Defines a rule to block emails sent to specific addresses using a case-insensitive regex pattern.                              |
| `score TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK 10.0`                                                                                 | Assigns a spam score of 10.0 to emails that match the rule `TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK`.                                              |
| `describe TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK Emails sent to blocked addresses are likely spam.`                                 | Provides a human-readable explanation of the rule's purpose, indicating that emails sent to blocked addresses are likely spam. |
config.example:
header TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK To =~ /^(mail@your.domain\\.io|mail@your.domain\\.org)$/i 
score TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK 10.0 
describe TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK Emails sent to blocked addresses are likely spam.

config.description:
The configuration settings for the mail server's spam filter using SpamAssassin. 
This includes rules and criteria for identifying and blocking spam emails.
The configuration is written in a domain-specific language that utilizes regular expressions
to specify conditions for email addresses that should be blocked or flagged as spam.
It also includes scoring mechanisms to determine the likelihood that an email is spam, 
and descriptions for each rule to explain its purpose and functionality.
Explanation of the example:
| Line                                                                                                             | Explanation                                                                                                                    |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `header TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK To =~ /^(mail@your.domain\\.io\|mail@your.domain\\.org)$/i`                          | Defines a rule to block emails sent to specific addresses using a case-insensitive regex pattern.                              |
| `score TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK 10.0`                                                                                 | Assigns a spam score of 10.0 to emails that match the rule `TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK`.                                              |
| `describe TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK Emails sent to blocked addresses are likely spam.`                                 | Provides a human-readable explanation of the rule's purpose, indicating that emails sent to blocked addresses are likely spam. |
config.example:
header TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK To =~ /^(mail@your.domain\\.io|mail@your.domain\\.org)$/i 
score TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK 10.0 
describe TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK Emails sent to blocked addresses are likely spam.

x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDRON_TOKEN" "https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/mailserver/spam_custom_config" --data '{"config":"header TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK To =~ /^(mail@your.domain\\.io|mail@your.domain\\.org)$/i score TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK 10.0 describe TO_ADDRESS_TO_BLOCK Emails sent to blocked addresses are likely spam."}'

get.description: Get the "Domain Name Sytem Blacklist" configurations.

zones.description:
An array of DNSBL zones to be queried by the mail server. 
Each zone represents a DNS-based blacklist that the server will use to check the origin of incoming emails. 
IP addresses found in these zones are typically associated with known spam sources. 
Mail servers query these DNSBL zones during the email validation process to determine the reputation of the sender's IP address.

post.description: Set the "Domain Name Sytem Blacklist" configurations.

zones.description:
An array of DNSBL zones to be queried by the mail server. 
Each zone represents a DNS-based blacklist that the server will use to check the origin of incoming emails. 
IP addresses found in these zones are typically associated with known spam sources. 
Mail servers query these DNSBL zones during the email validation process to determine the reputation of the sender's IP address.
**Existing domains also have to be inside that array, otherwise zones array will be replaced by the the one sent with this call.**

diskSize.description: The total disk space used by the mailbox, measured in bytes.

quotaValue.description: The current usage of the mailbox, measured in bytes.

quotaLimit.description: The maximum allowed usage of the mailbox, measured in bytes. A value of 0 indicates that there is no quota limit.

quotaPercent.description: The percentage of mailbox usage relative to the quota limit. If the quota limit is 0, this value will always be 0.

get.description:
Get the User's custom avatar (icon). This route is public. As a simple security measure, this requires the user id.
To display the user's avatar , use the `avatarUrl` property of the [profile](#operation/getProfile) endpoint. The `avatarUrl` will link to this endpoint when the user has a custom icon.

Get the user's custom background image for the dashboard.
Use the `hasBackgroundImage` property of the [profile](#operation/getProfile) endpoint to determine if the user has a custom background image.

post.description: Set the user's background image for the dashboard

Sets 2FA Secret. Enabling 2FA is a two step process:
  * Call this endpoint to generate a secret. The response contains the secret and a qrcode
  * Call the [Enable 2FA](#operation/enableTwoFactorAuthentication) endpoint with a TOTP Token (generated off the above secret)

Enables Two Factor Authentication. Enabling 2FA is a two step process:
  * Call the [Set 2FA Secret](#operation/setTwoFactorAuthenticationSecret) endpoint to generate a secret. The response contains the secret and a qrcode
  * Call this endpoint with a TOTP Token (generated off the above secret) to enable 2FA

totpToken.description: Current TOTP Token based off the [generate secret](#operation/setTwoFactorAuthenticationSecret)

post.description: API endpoint to configure domain settings. To read more about the existing domain provider and their domain configs inside the **Domain Route**.

provider.description: The provider of the TLS configuration.
The backup service provider (e.g., AWS, Azure).

format.description: The format of the backup file (e.g., tar, zip).

password.description: The password used to encrypt the backup.

encryptedFilenames.description: Enables or disables encrypted file names.

remotePath.description: The remote path where backups are stored (e.g., s3://mybucket/backups/).

version.description: The version of the provisioning script or configuration.

skipDnsSetup.description: Enable or disable to skip the DNS setup.

x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDRON_TOKEN" "https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/provision/restore" --data '{"backupConfig": {"provider": "aws53", "password": "strongPassword","format":"targz"}, "encryptedFilenames": true, "remotePath": "your.remote.path", "version": "v1.02.2", ipv4Config": {}, "ipv6Config": {}, "skipDnsSetup": false}'

username.description: The unique identifier for the user, typically used for login.

password.description: The user's password, used for authentication. This should be stored securely.

email.description: The user's email address, used for account recovery and notifications.

displayName.description: The name displayed publicly for the user, such as in user profiles or posts.

userId.description: The unique identifier associated with the user.

token.description: The authentication token used for user authentication.

expires.description: The expiration date and time of the authentication token.

post.description: Trigger manual renewal of Let's encrypt certificates. Note that certs are automatically renewed by Cloudron, 1 month before their expiry.

rebuild.description: When set all the nginx configurations are regenerated.

get.description: Gets the list of proxies in front of Cloudron. Cloudron will trust the various reverse proxy headers like `X-Forwarded-For` when requests originate from these proxy addresses.

post.description: Sets the list of proxies in front of Cloudron. Cloudron will trust the various reverse proxy headers like `X-Forwarded-For` when requests originate from these proxy addresses.

x-codeSamples.source: curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDRON_TOKEN" "https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/reverseproxy/trusted_ips" --data '{"trustedIps":"# My IPv4 Proxy\n10.244.0.0/16"}'

items.description: service

post.description: This call is used to configure a service.

memoryLimit.description: Limits the available memory. The unit used here is bytes.

recoveryMode.description: Enables or disables the recovery mode.

x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDRON_TOKEN" "https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/services/$SERVICE" -- data '{"memoryLimit":4294967296, "recoveryMode": false}'

parameters.description: Last lines displayed.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Defines the format of the logs.

schema.description: Each line in the returned logs is of the desired format. When the format is `json`, each line is a [NDJSON](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JSON_streaming#Newline-Delimited_JSON) containing the fields `realtimeTimestamp` and `message`. The `Content-Disposition` header is set to `attachment; filename="mail.log"`.

get.description: Get Service logs. Use this endpoint to stream logs (`tail -f` style). If you want to download logs, use the [logs](#operation/getServiceLogs) endpoint instead. This endpoint implements [Server Side Events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events). Use the [EventSource](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/EventSource) interface to parse the returned stream.

capacity.description: Fractional number of percentage the disk is in use.

contents.description: An array containing all units this disk holds, apps, volumes, built-in units like email

id.description: usage unit identifier could be appId or built-in types

usage.description: Disk space used by this unit in bytes

post.description: Disk usage is only updated automatically once per day. This API allows to trigger an update of that information.

List the API tokens created by the user. Note that tokens are personal (per user) and not global.

x-codeSamples.source: curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDRON_TOKEN" "https://$CLOUDRON_DOMAIN/api/v1/tokens" --data '{"name":"Token For Automation"}'

delete.description: Deletes the Token. All future requests using this token will instantly fail.

get.description: Gets information of any pending update to apps and the platform. This endpoint returns the cached update information and does not contact cloudron.io App Store. The update information could be stale and if you want the latest information use the [checkForUpdates](#operation/checkForUpdates) endpoint.

post.description: Gets information of any pending update to apps and the platform by contacting cloudron.io App Store. This call can take a while since it has to get the update information of each installed app. Use the [getUpdates](#operation/getUpdates) endpoint to get the update information since the last check.
Updates Cloudron to the next available release. The update is started asynchronously and can be monitored using the [tasks API](#tag/Tasks).

skipBackup.description: Whether backup should be skipped before performing the update.
